rows.case: EdgeEntityCooccur weight sums correctly within a single Compact pass (multiple log entries + existing graph.dat)
Non-cooccur edge types dedupe last-write-wins within a single pass
No edge log present is a no-op
Successful compaction truncates the logs it compacted
Crash (write failure) before WriteCSR's rename leaves graph.dat absent/unchanged and edge log fully intact
graph.dat durable and correct even when truncation fails after a successful write
TruncateNode in isolation: no-op on nonexistent node, resets to empty, works with/without an open writer
Retry after a truncate-phase failure does NOT double-count EdgeEntityCooccur weight, and the retry itself successfully finishes truncation
rows.empirically_confirmed_would_fail_pre_fix: Weight got 6, want 3 (verified by running this test against the pre-fix compact.go/edgelog.go via git stash)

rows.case: Compact-state sidecar absent (first-ever compaction, or graph.dat predates this fix) behaves identically to before (afterSeg=-1, read everything)
rows.covered_by: TestCompaction's subtests all start from a fresh temp dir with no sidecar present - implicitly covered

rows.case: Full module regression: no other package/test affected

commands_run: go test ./... -count=1 -timeout 300s (PASS, all engine module packages: engine, btree, catalog, graph, mvcc, split, wal)

git stash push -- graph/compact.go graph/edgelog.go; go test ./graph/... -run TestCompaction_RetryAfterTruncateFailureDoesNotDoubleCountWeight -v (FAIL, Weight=6 want 3, confirming the new test catches the pre-fix bug); git stash pop
